summary: Fixed SObjLib data/rodata modeling by replacing an over-broad synthetic SObjLibData aggregate with explicit globals, strings, padding, GObjFuncs, and HSD_Chan initializers. The PR also corrected the HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC signature to use u16 width/height, simplified casts accordingly, and updated GALE01 symbols to split one 0x78 data blob into two 0x30 channel structs plus a string. decomp.dev reported new matches for sobjlib .rodata and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC, plus improvements in .data and .sbss.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, changed lbl_8040C418 from a single 0x78 object to a 0x30 object, then added lbl_8040C448 as another 0x30 object and lbl_8040C478 as a 0x16 string.
In sobjlib.c, removed the local SObjLibData aggregate typedef that combined GObjFuncs, filename, error strings, jump table padding, two HSD_Chan values, and another error string into one struct.
Defined HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A0 as a GObjFunc array containing HSD_SObjLib_803A4740 and defined HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A4 as a real GObjFuncs initializer.
Changed HSD_SObjLib_804D7960 from an extern declaration to an actual u8 definition in sobjlib.c.
Added static char filename[] = "sobjlib.c" and static u8 data_pad_jumptable[0x3C] = { 0 } to preserve/data-match layout around known strings and jump table space.
Replaced uses of data-backed error-string externs/struct fields with direct string literals: "BadOBJ for SOBJ-displayfunc\n" and "Out CameraDisp Range\n".
Added two explicit static HSD_Chan initializers, lbl_8040C418 for GX_COLOR0 and lbl_8040C448 for GX_ALPHA0, and used them directly in HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC via HSD_SetupChannel.
Changed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC parameters from int width, int height to u16 width, u16 height in both sobjlib.c and sobjlib.h.
Initialized eye and interest vectors inline as { 0, 0, 1 } and { 0, 0, 0 } instead of loading extern Vec3 data symbols.
Included <sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h> in sobjlib.h to support the exposed GObjFuncs declaration.
smart_moves: Split the old inferred 0x78 data region into two typed HSD_Chan objects, matching the symbol table sizes and making HSD_SetupChannel calls use real typed data instead of struct-field offsets.
Used explicit static data objects and padding to model the data section order without forcing unrelated objects into a fake monolithic struct.
Changing width and height to u16 let the function naturally express the same truncation that had previously been forced with casts, helping HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC reach a reported 100% match.
Inlining small Vec3 constants and OSReport strings avoided unnecessary extern data symbols while preserving the expected rodata/data layout.
Kept symbol metadata synchronized with source changes, especially the split of lbl_8040C418/lbl_8040C448/lbl_8040C478.
decomp_lessons: Do not overfit adjacent data into a single synthetic struct when the real layout is several independent globals; explicit typed objects plus named padding can improve both readability and matching.
If a function takes dimensions that are semantically 16-bit, changing the prototype to u16 can remove noisy casts and fix codegen around arithmetic conversions.
For data-section matching, update symbols.txt at the same time as source declarations so object boundaries, sizes, and string annotations reflect the reconstructed layout.
HSD_Chan initializers should be represented as real HSD_Chan globals when channel setup code takes their addresses directly.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for small data-layout PRs: this one showed .rodata becoming 100%, HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC becoming 100%, and .data/.sbss improving.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data labels retained address-style names such as lbl_8040C418 and lbl_8040C448.
SObjLib globals keep the project’s address-derived HSD_SObjLib_80xxxxxx naming style until better semantic names are known.
Temporary layout-preserving padding was named descriptively as data_pad_jumptable.
The file-local source filename string was named filename.
Function symbols remain address-style names such as HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC and HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced an artificial aggregate data struct with separate definitions to match actual .data object boundaries.
Used static zero padding of size 0x3C to occupy the jump-table-sized gap in data layout.
Corrected parameter types to u16 to avoid explicit cast patterns for width/height and improve HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC codegen.
Initialized Vec3 constants locally rather than loading them from extern data symbols.
Represented error messages as string literals at use sites, contributing to the reported .rodata match.
Declared HSD_SObjLib_804D7960 and HSD_ObjAllocData storage in the C file to improve .sbss/.bss-style data matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the available slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increasing by 444 bytes and matched data increasing by 24 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported two new matches: sobjlib .rodata at 100% (+24 bytes) and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC at 100% (+1 byte).
decomp.dev bot reported unmatched-item improvements for sobjlib .data (+115 bytes, 48.07%) and .sbss (+4 bytes, 50.00%).
follow_up_queries: Find other baselib files using large synthetic data structs that may actually be adjacent globals.
Search for functions with int width/height parameters but repeated u16 casts that may need u16 prototypes.
Audit nearby symbols around jumptable_8040C3DC and lbl_8040C418 for remaining data-boundary mistakes.
Check whether lbl_8040C418 and lbl_8040C448 can receive semantic channel names once surrounding SObjLib behavior is understood.
